questions.question: What is matter?
questions.correct_answer: Anything that occupies space and has mass
questions.explanation: Everything in the universe is made up of material which scientists have named matter.

questions.question: What are the three physical states of matter?
questions.explanation: Matter exists in these three states due to variation in the characteristics of its particles.

questions.question: What is diffusion?
questions.correct_answer: The intermixing of particles of two different types of matter on their own
questions.explanation: Particles of matter have space between them and they get into the spaces of others.

questions.question: How does temperature affect kinetic energy?
questions.correct_answer: As temperature rises, kinetic energy of particles increases
questions.explanation: With an increase in temperature, particles move faster.

questions.question: What is the melting point?
questions.correct_answer: The minimum temperature at which a solid melts to become a liquid at atmospheric pressure
questions.explanation: It is an indication of the strength of the force of attraction between particles.

questions.question: What is latent heat of fusion?
questions.correct_answer: The amount of heat energy required to change 1 kg of a solid into liquid at atmospheric pressure at its melting point
questions.explanation: The word latent means hidden; this heat is used to overcome forces of attraction.

questions.question: What is sublimation?
questions.correct_answer: A change of state directly from solid to gas without changing into liquid state
questions.explanation: Common examples include camphor or ammonium chloride.

questions.question: What is evaporation?
questions.correct_answer: The phenomenon of change of a liquid into vapours at any temperature below its boiling point
questions.explanation: It is a surface phenomenon where particles gain enough energy to break away.

questions.question: Why does evaporation cause cooling?
questions.correct_answer: Particles absorb energy from the surroundings to regain the energy lost during evaporation
questions.explanation: The absorption of energy from the surroundings makes the surroundings cold.

questions.question: What is dry ice?
questions.explanation: It is stored under high pressure and becomes a gas directly when pressure is reduced to 1 atmosphere.

notes.title: Particle Nature
notes.content: Matter is made of tiny particles; they have spaces between them and attract each other.

notes.title: State Characteristics
notes.content: Solids have definite shape/volume. Liquids have fixed volume but no fixed shape. Gases have neither.

notes.title: Effect of Pressure
notes.content: Applying pressure and reducing temperature can liquefy gases by bringing particles closer.

notes.title: Factors affecting Evaporation
notes.content: Evaporation increases with surface area, temperature, and wind speed; it decreases with humidity.

facts.fact: The SI unit of mass is kilogram (kg).
The SI unit of volume is cubic metre (m³).
Potassium permanganate crystals can color a large volume of water due to millions of tiny particles.
Gases like LPG and Oxygen in hospitals are supplied in compressed form due to high compressibility.
